steps.title: Trace the text's two movements
steps.content: The passage opens with Charlot delivering Atlante's letter to Rinaldo using a split cane — a physical, mechanical act. It then shifts to Rinaldo's emotional reaction: he is "transported with joy" upon reading the letter. Any correct structure description must capture both parts in order.

steps.title: Test each choice against the actual structure
steps.content:
The correct choice must describe (1) the letter delivery, then (2) a character's happiness at reading it.
  • "It describes the delivery of a letter, and then portrays a character's happiness at reading that letter.": matches both movements exactly — the cane delivery, then Rinaldo's joy. ✓
  • "It establishes that a character is desperate to receive a letter, and then explains why another character has not yet written that letter.": Rinaldo is melancholy, not desperate, and the letter has already been written; neither element appears in the text. ✗
  • "It presents a character's concerns about delivering a letter, and then details the contents of that letter.": Charlot worries about the letter falling, but the text never reveals the letter's contents. ✗
  • "It reveals the inspiration behind a character's letter, and then emphasizes the excitement that another character feels upon receiving that letter.": Atlante's inspiration for writing is never discussed; the text begins with the delivery, not the motivation. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
